description: Trigger discrimination for whole-repo scan orchestration + aggregation (this skill) vs the finding-triage JUDGMENT it yields to (static-analysis-reviewer), the dependency/provenance judgment it folds in but does not make (supply-chain-security-reviewer), the in-batch SAST-run design it aggregates (sast-orchestration-designer, pinned both ways), the dynamic running-app testing it excludes (dast-safety-harness-designer), and the pipeline it produces a contract for (ci-pipeline-architect).

cases.prompt: Coordinate a security scan across the whole repo — the code analyzer, the dependency/SCA scanner, the secret scanner, and the infra-config scanner — capture what ran and what failed, dedup findings that show up in more than one tool, put severities on one scale, and hand me a single report.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — coordinating the multi-category suite, capturing coverage/gaps, cross-tool normalization/dedup, and one aggregated report ARE this skill's four parts. No triage verdict, no single-tool run design, no live-app testing is requested.
cases.reason: The subject is running and aggregating the whole-repo scan into one report. It yields the finding triage to static-analysis-reviewer and orchestrates (not judges) the dependency scan.

cases.prompt: Take this scanner output and work out which findings are true positives, which are false positives, and rank the real ones by reachability and impact.
cases.note: ORCHESTRATE-vs-TRIAGE SEAM (mandatory) — deciding what findings MEAN, sorting TP/FP, and ranking is the triage JUDGMENT. This skill produces the findings; static-analysis-reviewer judges them.
cases.reason: Interpreting and ranking findings is static-analysis-reviewer. security-scan-orchestrator RUNS and AGGREGATES the scan and hands the findings to triage; it never decides their disposition.

cases.prompt: Design how our SAST specifically should run over this repo — which analyzers per language, ruleset config, a baseline plus diff-scanning that only gates new findings on a PR, and a governed false-positive suppression list.
cases.note: IN-BATCH SEAM (pinned both ways) — designing the SAST RUN (selection, config, baseline/diff, governed suppression) is one category's run design. This skill aggregates that output with the other categories into the whole-repo report.
cases.reason: SAST-run design is sast-orchestration-designer. security-scan-orchestrator coordinates the whole suite and folds the SAST results into one aggregate; it does not design the SAST run.

cases.prompt: Look at our dependencies and lockfile — which CVEs actually matter given what's reachable, is anything typosquatted or unpinned, and are the install/build scripts safe?
cases.reason: Judging dependency/provenance risk (reachability, typosquat, pinning, install scripts) is supply-chain-security-reviewer. security-scan-orchestrator orchestrates the dependency-scan RUN and includes its output in the aggregate; it does not make the supply-chain judgment.

cases.prompt: We want to security-test the running application in staging — send test traffic at the live app and see what holds up.
cases.note: STATIC-vs-DYNAMIC SEAM — testing a RUNNING app is dynamic and carries a written-authorization gate. This skill's suite is static (repo) scanning only.
cases.reason: Dynamic running-app testing is dast-safety-harness-designer (authorization + safety harness). security-scan-orchestrator scans the repo statically and does not fold live-app testing into that.

cases.prompt: Design our CI delivery pipeline end to end — the stage graph, merge-blocking rules, secret governance, and deploy gates.
cases.reason: Designing the delivery pipeline is ci-pipeline-architect. security-scan-orchestrator produces the scan/report contract that a pipeline's security stage runs; it does not design the pipeline.
